title: integration tests: Permission Denied with GOPATH != /go
body:
# **kind bug**

**Description**

When running integration tests on ubuntu 18, we see gobs of 'permission denied' errors.  @baude and I looked into this for a few hours and concluded, somehow/why this is related to ``$GOPATH`` not being ``/go``  (in CI's case, ``/root/go``).  Since the test is running as root, the error didn't make much sense.  At the time, getting CI running was our goal, so we left a deeper investigation up to this issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Modify ``.cirrus.yml`` environment variable``$CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR`` to ``/root//go/src/github.com/containers/libpod``  (This is used to set ``$GOSRC``).

2. Modify ``contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh`` loop item ``"export GOPATH=\"/go\"" \`` -> ``"export GOPATH=\"/root/go\"" \``

3. Temporarily commit those changes and open a PR (so cirrus ci runs) -or- perform the equivalent steps in your dev. environment.

**Describe the results you received:**

Gobs of 'permission denied' errors when integration tests run (``contrib/cirrus/integration_test.sh``).

**Describe the results you expected:**

Integration tests should pass no matter what ``$GOPATH`` (and ``$CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR``) are set to.

comments.body: podman --priviliged command is still being blocked by SELinux.  podman 0.9.3.1 has this issue.  Might explain why this suddenly started showing up?

This is Ubuntu and we tried disabling AppArmor, though it's also entirely possible we did that wrong.  While reading about AppArmor, there were a few side-notes that concerned us.  One being something about paths being incorrect when running inside a chroot (which ***is*** happening), and another I can't remember.  Will paste link if I can find it...
[...oh here it is.](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/DebuggingApparmor#AppArmor_and_chroot_environments).  I'll bet this affects buildah too, eh?
Yes, and even if not, IIUC it's CI-specific and we've got (obviously) functional workarounds.
